summary: The session focused on Greek mythology, particularly the stories of Medea, Jason, Hercules, and Eurystheus. The user requested definitions of various characters and their roles, as well as a side story about Alcides' origin and the Twelve Labors assigned to him.
facts: Medea tricks the daughters of Pelias into slicing him up.
Jason seeks to be with Medea but is ultimately portrayed as a villain.
Hercules, also known as Heracles, is considered the best of the demigods and is known for his strength and foolishness.
Zeus starts a war to obtain Amphitryon, disguising himself and fathering a child with Alcmene.
Hercules must perform 12 labors as punishment for killing his family in a fit of madness.
The 12 labors are designed to kill, humiliate, and exhaust Hercules.
Hercules completes all 12 labors and earns forgiveness for his guilt.
Deianeria is attacked by the centaur Nessus, whom Hercules kills with a poison arrow.
Nessus gives Deianeria a love potion before dying, which ultimately causes Hercules pain when he believes she hates him.
Eurystheus is the king who assigns Hercules the Twelve Labors as punishment for his actions.
Alcides, also known as Hercules, is the son of Zeus and Alcmene, and is targeted by Hera due to jealousy.
Hera attempts to kill Hercules as a baby, leading to the creation of the Twelve Labors.
